results.prompt: Recommend free resume builders that let me export PDF without forcing paid plans.
results.responseText:
Certainly! Here are five free resume builders that allow you to export your resume as a PDF without requiring a paid plan:

1. **CV Studio**: Offers a 100% free resume builder with no sign-up or payment required. You can upload your existing resume or start from scratch, customize it, and export it in PDF or DOCX formats. Additionally, it includes an ATS score checker to help your resume pass applicant tracking systems. ([cvstudio.org](https://www.cvstudio.org/?utm_source=openai))

2. **Google Docs**: Provides a variety of professional resume templates that are completely free to use. You can edit and export your resume as a PDF without any limitations. This option is ideal for those who prefer a straightforward, no-frills approach to resume building. ([mortit.com](https://mortit.com/blog/best-free-resume-builders?utm_source=openai))

3. **ResuFit**: Offers a free plan that includes a full resume editor, CV analysis, and PDF downloads without requiring a credit card. It also provides AI-powered tailoring to specific job descriptions, enhancing your resume's relevance. ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-truly-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-costs-or-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

4. **OpenResume**: An open-source resume builder that is completely free to use. It offers a single, highly customizable template focused on the U.S. job market, with ATS optimization built in. Your data stays in your browser, ensuring maximum privacy. ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-truly-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-costs-or-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Indeed Resume Builder**: Allows you to create and download a resume as a PDF without any hidden fees. It integrates directly with Indeed job applications, making it convenient for users applying through the platform. ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-truly-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-costs-or-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

All these tools are free to use and allow PDF exports without requiring a paid plan. They also prioritize user privacy, with some offering local-only storage options. However, it's important to note that while these platforms are free, they may have limitations in terms of template variety, customization options, or additional features. Always review each platform's offerings to ensure they meet your specific needs.

results.prompt: I want a CV builder where I can keep data local in my browser. What tools should I use?
results.responseText:
Here are 5 ranked CV/resume builders that allow you to keep your data local in your browser, ensuring privacy and control:

1. **Resumake.io**  
   - Free and open-source  
   - Works entirely in your browser, no data sent to servers  
   - ATS-friendly PDF output  
   - Privacy: Local storage only, no cloud upload  
   - Link: https://resumake.io/

2. **Reactive Resume**  
   - Open-source and free  
   - Fully functional offline in the browser  
   - ATS-compatible export formats (PDF, JSON)  
   - Privacy: Data stored locally, optional cloud sync available but not required  
   - Link: https://rxresu.me/

3. **Standard Resume**  
   - Free tier available; premium features at a cost  
   - Allows local data saving and editing offline  
   - Clean, ATS-compliant designs  
   - Privacy: Data saved locally; cloud sync optional  
   - Link: https://standardresume.co/

4. **Novoresume** (Free plan with local option)  
   - Free basic plan  
   - Supports browser-based editing, but cloud save can be avoided by downloading frequently  
   - ATS-friendly templates  
   - Privacy: Primarily cloud but manual local save possible  
   - Link: https://novoresume.com/

5. **JSON Resume**  
   - Developer-friendly, open-source schema and tools  
   - Edit resumes in JSON format locally with offline tools  
   - High customizability and ATS-friendly outputs  
   - Privacy: Fully local unless you use their hosting service  
   - Link: https://jsonresume.org/

These tools prioritize local data storage and privacy, with clean and ATS-friendly designs. Resumake.io and Reactive Resume stand out for purely local use without cloud dependence.

results.prompt: Which resume tools support local-first editing and optional cloud sync?
results.responseText:
Here are five resume builders that support local-first editing with optional cloud synchronization:

1. **HeyCV**: A free, privacy-focused resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It offers optional cloud synchronization, allowing you to access your resume from any device. No sign-up is required, and you can export your resume to PDF. ([heycv.app](https://heycv.app/?utm_source=openai))

2. **ATS Resume Maker**: This open-source, local-first resume builder ensures your data remains on your device, with no cloud storage by default. It offers optional GitHub synchronization for multi-device access, providing version control and backup. No account is required, and it complies with GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA regulations. ([ats-resume-maker.com](https://ats-resume-maker.com/features/local-first/?utm_source=openai))

3. **VitaeKit**: A free resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It offers optional cloud synchronization through account creation, allowing you to access your resume from any device. No sign-up is required for basic use, and you can export your resume to PDF or Word. ([vitaekit.com](https://vitaekit.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **My CV**: A free, privacy-focused resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It works offline and can be installed as a Progressive Web App (PWA) for easy access. No sign-up is required, and you can export your resume to PDF. ([my-cv.click](https://my-cv.click/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Local CV**: An open-source, privacy-first resume builder that saves your data locally on your device. It offers optional cloud synchronization through account creation, allowing you to access your resume from any device. No sign-up is required for basic use, and you can export your resume to PDF. ([localcv.tusharvashishth.com](https://localcv.tusharvashishth.com/?utm_source=openai))

These tools prioritize your privacy by storing your data locally, with optional cloud synchronization for multi-device access. They offer free services with no hidden fees, and most provide ATS-friendly templates to enhance your resume's compatibility with applicant tracking systems.

results.prompt: Top ATS resume builders for software engineers, with template variety and PDF export.
results.responseText:
Here are five top ATS-friendly resume builders suitable for software engineers, offering a variety of templates and PDF export capabilities:

1. **StackInterview Resume Builder**
   - **Features**: Offers 15 professionally designed, ATS-safe templates tailored for software engineers, including FAANG-ready layouts.
   - **Template Variety**: Includes both single-column and two-column designs.
   - **Export Options**: Allows direct PDF export without requiring an account.
   - **Privacy**: Data is stored locally in your browser, ensuring privacy.
   - **Link**: ([stackinterview.dev](https://stackinterview.dev/resume-builder/templates?utm_source=openai))

2. **Professional Resume Free**
   - **Features**: Provides over 46 industry-specific, ATS-optimized templates with 12 free optimization tools.
   - **Template Variety**: Offers a wide range of templates across various industries.
   - **Export Options**: Enables instant PDF downloads without sign-up or watermarks.
   - **Privacy**: No sign-up required, enhancing user privacy.
   - **Link**: ([professionalresumefree.com](https://www.professionalresumefree.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Reactive Resume**
   - **Features**: An open-source, privacy-focused resume builder with multiple templates and a clean, minimalistic design.
   - **Template Variety**: Offers several templates suitable for various professions.
   - **Export Options**: Supports PDF export and allows self-hosting for enhanced privacy.
   - **Privacy**: Data is stored locally or can be self-hosted, ensuring full control over your information.
   - **Link**: ([resumemd.pro](https://resumemd.pro/blog/best-free-resume-builders-2026?utm_source=openai))

4. **CV Maker**
   - **Features**: Provides free resume builders, CV templates, and ATS-friendly exports.
   - **Template Variety**: Offers a selection of clean and professional templates.
   - **Export Options**: Allows export to PDF and Word formats.
   - **Privacy**: Data is stored locally in your browser, ensuring privacy.
   - **Link**: ([imageconver.com](https://imageconver.com/cv-maker/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Google Docs Resume Templates**
   - **Features**: Offers a variety of free, clean, and simple resume templates.
   - **Template Variety**: Provides several basic templates suitable for ATS parsing.
   - **Export Options**: Supports PDF export and easy sharing.
   - **Privacy**: Data is stored in your Google account, with standard privacy controls.
   - **Link**: ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/the-ultimate-guide-to-truly-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-costs-or-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

These tools are designed to help software engineers create professional, ATS-friendly resumes with ease, offering a range of templates and export options to suit various needs.

results.prompt: Best CV builder for UK job applications with professional templates and no paywall.
What free CV builders work well for London/UK job seekers?
Compare Canva CV templates vs dedicated CV builders for speed and ATS quality.
results.responseText:
Here's a comparison between Canva CV templates and dedicated CV builders focusing on speed and ATS quality:

1. **Speed:**
   - **Canva CV Templates:** Canva offers a highly visual drag-and-drop interface that can be very fast for users comfortable with design tools. Creating a CV is intuitive with plenty of customization options, but this can sometimes lead to spending extra time tweaking design elements.
   - **Dedicated CV Builders:** These tools are designed specifically for CV creation, often providing step-by-step guidance, pre-written content suggestions, and automatic formatting. This usually results in faster completion times, especially for users less confident in design.

2. **ATS Quality:**
   - **Canva CV Templates:** Canva templates are often rich in graphics, varied fonts, and layout elements, which can make parsing by Applicant Tracking Systems less reliable. While visually appealing, many Canva CVs are not fully optimized for ATS readability unless specifically designed with simplicity in mind.
   - **Dedicated CV Builders:** Most dedicated builders prioritize ATS-friendly output, using simple, structured, and standardized formats that ATS algorithms can easily parse. They often offer tips to optimize your CV for ATS, improving chances of passing through initial automated screening.

3. **Privacy:**
   - **Canva:** Cloud-based, meaning your CV is stored on Canva's servers. Offers good security but involves trusting a third party with your personal info. You can download local copies, but editing typically happens online.
   - **Dedicated CV Builders:** Varies — some are cloud-based with similar privacy tradeoffs, but others offer downloadable templates or local editing options. Always check individual privacy policies.

### Summary:
- Use **Canva** if you want creative control and a visually striking CV quickly, but be cautious about ATS compatibility.
- Use **Dedicated CV Builders** if you prioritize speed, tailored content suggestions, and ensuring ATS friendliness.

Would you like me to recommend some top dedicated CV builders that balance speed, ATS quality, and privacy?

results.prompt: I want template switching plus real-time preview for resumes. Best free options?
Recommend resume builders with good privacy and minimal tracking concerns.
results.responseText:
Here are five resume builders that prioritize privacy and minimize tracking concerns:

1. **PrivaFolio**: A free, browser-based resume builder that stores all data locally, ensuring complete privacy. No accounts or cloud storage are required, and it offers features like easy editing, secure sharing with custom expiration dates, and polished PDF exports. ([privafolio.com](https://www.privafolio.com/?utm_source=openai))

2. **PrivateCV**: This privacy-first resume creator operates entirely offline, with no servers or data storage, keeping your information secure. It provides ATS-friendly templates, real-time editing, and easy export options. ([privatecv.vercel.app](https://privatecv.vercel.app/?utm_source=openai))

3. **VertoCV**: A free, privacy-focused resume builder that stores your data exclusively in your browser. It offers ATS-optimized templates, offline functionality, and easy export as PDF or JSON. ([vertocv.com](https://vertocv.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Private Resume Builder**: This tool allows you to create and tailor your resume without an account, storing all data locally. It features ATS resume checking, professional templates, and high-quality PDF exports. ([privatecv.app](https://www.privatecv.app/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Local-First Architecture - ATS Resume Maker**: A resume builder that operates entirely offline, ensuring your data remains on your device. It offers ATS-optimized templates, local storage, and optional GitHub sync for multi-device access. ([ats-resume-maker.com](https://ats-resume-maker.com/features/local-first/?utm_source=openai))

These tools emphasize user privacy by keeping your data local and avoiding unnecessary tracking or cloud storage.

results.prompt: Have you heard of Dossier CV Builder? Is it legit and useful?
results.responseText:
Dossier CV Builder is a resume creation tool offered by Dossier, a company that provides various professional services. While specific user reviews for Dossier CV Builder are limited, the company emphasizes user privacy and data protection. According to their privacy policy, Dossier collects personal and professional information when users engage with their products or subscribe to communications. They state that they protect user information, do not share it except for very limited purposes, and provide users with the right to be forgotten. ([dossier.work](https://dossier.work/privacy/?utm_source=openai))

Given the limited availability of user reviews and detailed information about Dossier CV Builder, it's advisable to exercise caution when using the service. If you have concerns about data privacy or the tool's functionality, consider exploring alternative resume builders with more established reputations and user feedback.

results.prompt: What is the fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF?
results.responseText:
The fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF typically involves the following steps:

1. Choose a CV/resume builder that supports quick input of bullet points and easy formatting.
2. Copy and paste or import your existing experience bullets directly into the builder.
3. Use pre-made templates to quickly format the CV without manual design work.
4. Review and adjust the sections for consistency and clarity.
5. Export or download the CV as a PDF file directly from the builder.

For speed and ease, it's best to use an online CV builder with import functionality or a tool that supports quick editing of bullet points and templates. 

Would you like me to recommend specific tools that match these criteria?

results.prompt: I need a free CV builder with import, edit, preview, and PDF download. Top options?
results.responseText:
Here are five free CV builders that offer import, edit, preview, and PDF download features:

1. **CV Studio**: Upload your existing resume or paste your resume text to get started. No signup required. ATS-optimized templates. Export to PDF and DOCX. ([cvstudio.org](https://www.cvstudio.org/?utm_source=openai))

2. **MyPDFCV**: Build professional resumes with real-time preview, 7 templates, and one-click PDF export in multiple languages. No paywall, no sign-up, no data stored on servers. ([mypdfcv.com](https://www.mypdfcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Make.CV**: Create professional resumes with real-time preview, 7 templates, and one-click PDF export in multiple languages. No paywall, no sign-up, no data stored on servers. ([make.cv](https://make.cv/?utm_source=openai))

4. **FlowCV**: Create a professional resume in minutes with 50+ templates. Import existing resumes, customize layouts, and download unlimited PDFs. ([flowcv.com](https://flowcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

5. **MojCV**: Choose from professional, ATS-optimized templates. No credit card required, no free trials. Download unlimited, watermark-free PDFs. ([mojcv.com](https://mojcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

All these platforms are free to use and provide essential features for creating and downloading professional CVs.
